name: Google Chromebook (coreboot + Cr50 / Ti50)
description: Google Chromebooks use coreboot firmware with a Google-designed security chip (Cr50 for older ARM/Intel Chromebooks; Ti50 for recent Chromebooks). CRTM_VERSION identifies the coreboot build.
secure-boot-default: Google Verified Boot (not UEFI Secure Boot — uses chained signatures anchored in Cr50/Ti50)
ek-root-ca-source: Google publishes the Chromebook attestation root CA via pki.goog/ and the `vpd` (Vital Product Data) tool on Chromebooks; per-device certs are derived from Cr50/Ti50.
platforms-note: Chromebook CRTM strings often encode the code-name + date, e.g. 'Google_Dalboz.14675.0.0-rc1'. Map the code-name against the ChromeOS device catalogue at https://www.chromium.org/chromium-os/developer-information-for-chrome-os-devices/.
pluton-like-architecture: Cr50/Ti50 are Google's answer to Pluton: a dedicated microcontroller (H1 for Cr50, Titan M-like for Ti50) that performs the TPM 2.0 implementation in a hardware-isolated enclave with measured-boot anchored in the chip's ROM.
notes: Chromebooks don't use standard UEFI Secure Boot — they use Google's Verified Boot (coreboot → depthcharge → kernel, each link anchored in keys stored in the Cr50/Ti50). CRTM events are emitted by coreboot; the Cr50/Ti50 also provides a TPM 2.0 interface to the OS.
source: coreboot source tree; ChromeOS firmware images; Google Pixel / Chromebook security whitepapers.
